name: a real-time design couples a realtime application service to dedicated websocket cloud infrastructure
command:
printf '%s' '{"complete":true,"answers":{"motivation":"revenue","availability_tolerance":"minutes","data_loss_tolerance":"minutes","budget_posture":"balanced","data_volume":"large","read_write_pattern":"balanced","data_cadence":"real-time","criticality":"mission-critical","data_sensitivity":"confidential","compliance_regime":"none","scale":"large","consistency_need":"strong","communication_style":"event-driven","integration_scope":"public","workload":"a real-time collaboration app"}}' > p.json
bash $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/architect-session.sh --vision "a real-time collaboration app" --profile p.json --out-dir s --now 2026-06-08T00:00:00Z 2>/dev/null
bash $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/codesign-build.sh --requirements s/technical-requirements.json --platform aws --json 2>/dev/null > plan.json
node -e 'const p=require("./plan.json");const rt=p.app_units.find(x=>x.unit==="realtime-service");const ws=p.infra_units.find(x=>/websocket/.test(x.unit));process.exit(rt&&ws&&ws.serves_app.includes("realtime-service")?0:1)' && echo realtime-coupled 1>&2
